question: How does Excalidraw render and update canvas elements?

graph.answer:
Excalidraw uses a layered canvas pipeline, not direct DOM drawing.

- `Scene` is the source of truth for elements. When elements are replaced or mutated, `Scene.replaceAllElements()` rebuilds its maps and calls `triggerUpdate()`, which regenerates the scene nonce and notifies listeners; `App` subscribes with `this.scene.onUpdate(this.triggerRender)` so any scene change can schedule a rerender. See [Scene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/Scene.ts#L271) and [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L3235).

- The actual drawing is split into three layers:
  - `StaticCanvas` renders the main scene contents.
  - `InteractiveCanvas` renders UI overlays like selections, handles, snap lines, remote cursors, and scrollbars.
  - `NewElementCanvas` renders the in-progress element while you are drawing or resizing. See [StaticCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/StaticCanvas.tsx#L33), [InteractiveCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/InteractiveCanvas.tsx#L86), and [NewElementCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/NewElementCanvas.tsx#L25).

- React rerenders are intentionally gated. Both canvas components are `React.memo`’d with custom equality checks keyed on `canvasNonce`, `selectionNonce`, the element maps, visible elements, and the relevant subset of `appState`, so they only repaint when something that affects drawing actually changed. See [StaticCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/StaticCanvas.tsx#L108) and [InteractiveCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/InteractiveCanvas.tsx#L275).

- `StaticCanvas`’s effect sizes the `<canvas>` and calls `renderStaticScene()`. That renderer bootstraps the 2D context, draws the grid, then walks the visible elements and calls `renderElement()` for each one. `renderStaticScene()` can be throttled with `throttleRAF()` to avoid redundant work. See [staticScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/staticScene.ts#L236) and [staticScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/staticScene.ts#L498).

- `renderElement()` is the low-level element painter. It handles special cases like frames, freedraw, text, images, arrows, embeddables, and export mode, and it uses an offscreen-per-element cache for many shapes via `generateElementWithCanvas()` / `drawElementFromCanvas()`. That cache is invalidated when things like zoom, theme, bound text, image crop, or frame opacity change. See [renderElement.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/renderElement.ts#L781) and [renderElement.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/renderElement.ts#L609).

- `InteractiveCanvas` drives `renderInteractiveScene()`, which paints the non-scene UI layer. It also keeps a render loop alive through `AnimationController.start(...)` for transient animated effects, and it reports things like scrollbar geometry back to `App` through `renderInteractiveSceneCallback`. See [interactiveScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/interactiveScene.ts#L2031) and [interactiveScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/interactiveScene.ts#L2081), plus [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L3662).

In practice, the update path is:

1. A scene mutation happens through `updateScene()`, direct `scene.replaceAllElements()`, or element mutation APIs.
2. `Scene.triggerUpdate()` fires.
3. `App`’s `triggerRender()` runs and React state changes propagate.
4. The relevant canvas component sees changed props and repaints.
5. `StaticCanvas` redraws the scene; `InteractiveCanvas` redraws overlay UI; `NewElementCanvas` redraws the live draft element.

If you want, I can trace one specific interaction end-to-end, like pointer drag, undo/redo, or remote collaborator updates.
